preSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

## Coordination
- Read COORDINATION.md before starting any work
- Update COORDINATION.md after completing your work with:
  - What you did
  - Key decisions and their rationale
  - Warnings or context for the next agent
  - Status of your assigned tasks
preSessionState.COORDINATION.md:
# Coordination Log

Use this file to share decisions, status updates, and context with other agents.
Write your updates below. There is no enforced format — use whatever structure helps.

---

## Agent 2 — SMS and Webhook notification channels

**What I did:**
- Added `src/services/sms-channel.service.ts` — SMS channel handler that subscribes to EventBus for `order:created` and `order:status-changed` events
- Added `src/services/webhook-channel.service.ts` — Webhook channel handler that subscribes to EventBus for the same events, delivers payloads to registered webhook URLs
- Added `tests/services/sms-channel.service.test.ts` (7 tests) and `tests/services/webhook-channel.service.test.ts` (10 tests)

**Key decisions:**
- Both new handlers follow the same `eventBus.on<T>(eventType, handler)` subscription pattern used in `NotificationService`
- Both handlers subscribe to EventBus directly (not via CallbackRegistry), since the task was to extend the EventBus pattern
- SMS uses a `resolvePhoneNumber()` helper that derives a phone number from user ID (placeholder for real lookup)
- Webhook service has a URL registration model — webhooks only fire to registered URLs
- Did NOT modify existing `notification.service.ts` or `event-bus.ts`

**All 87 tests pass (70 existing + 17 new).**

## Agent 3 — Priority-based notification routing

**Requirement change acknowledgment:**
The previous broadcast approach (all channels subscribe to all EventBus events independently) is now invalidated. The new stakeholder requirement is priority-based routing where each notification goes to exactly one channel based on priority:
- urgent → SMS channel
- normal → email channel
- low → webhook channel

**What I did:**
- Created `src/services/priority-router.ts` — PriorityRouter class that accepts a `PriorityNotification` (with a `priority` field of `'urgent' | 'normal' | 'low'`) and routes to exactly one channel handler
- Added public `send()` methods to `SmsChannelService` and `WebhookChannelService` so the router can dispatch to them directly
- Added public `send()` method to `NotificationService` (email channel) for the same reason
- Updated `NotificationService` with `setPriorityRouter()` — when a router is set, events are routed by priority instead of always going to email:
  - Order creation priority is based on order total (≥$100 urgent, ≥$25 normal, <$25 low)
  - Status change priority is based on new status (cancelled=urgent, shipped/delivered=normal, other=low)
- Updated `src/index.ts` to export PriorityRouter and related types
- Added `tests/services/priority-router.test.ts` with 16 tests covering:
  - Direct routing for each priority level (urgent→SMS, normal→email, low→webhook)
  - `channelForPriority()` mapping
  - Default values (phone derivation, webhook URL/eventType)
  - Webhook metadata inclusion
  - Integration with NotificationService for event-driven routing through the priority router

**Key decisions:**
- The PriorityRouter is opt-in via `setPriorityRouter()` — existing code without the router still works (backward compatible)
- Channel handlers are preserved as-is; only added public `send()` methods
- The exhaustive switch in the router ensures type safety — adding a new priority level will cause a compile error
- Existing channel EventBus subscriptions are preserved — they can still be used for broadcast scenarios, but the priority router provides the new routing layer

**All 103 tests pass (87 existing + 16 new).**
